---
name: release-companion
description: Cut a new release of the Tauri companion app — bump versions, generate CHANGELOG, tag, push to trigger CI build matrix. Use when the user asks to "release the companion", "ship companion vX.Y.Z", or "tag a companion version".
---

# Release the companion app

The Tauri companion ships via GitHub Releases built by
`.github/workflows/release-companion.yml` (matrix Win / macOS-universal /
Ubuntu 22.04). Releases are gated on a `companion-vX.Y.Z` tag.

## 1. Pre-flight

- Working tree must be clean (`git status` shows nothing uncommitted).
- You're on `main` (or a release branch) and rebased.
- `pnpm typecheck` is green.
- The companion has actually changed since the last release —
  `git log --oneline 'companion-vX.Y.Z..HEAD' -- apps/companion packages/{protocol,sdk,types}`
  shows commits.

## 2. Pick a bump kind

The release script supports:

```pwsh
pnpm release:companion           # patch (default)
pnpm release:companion:minor
pnpm release:companion:major
pnpm release:companion <exact>   # e.g. 0.4.0-beta.1
```

Use SemVer:

- **patch** — bug fixes, no UX change.
- **minor** — new feature, backward compatible.
- **major** — breaking pairing / hub envelope / capability changes.

## 3. Run the release script

```pwsh
pnpm release:companion:minor
```

What it does (`scripts/release-companion.mjs`):

1. Bumps `version` in `apps/companion/package.json`,
   `apps/companion/src-tauri/Cargo.toml`,
   `apps/companion/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`.
2. Generates a CHANGELOG entry from conventional commits since the
   previous tag, scoped to `apps/companion` + `packages/{protocol,sdk,types}`.
3. Commits as `chore(companion): release vX.Y.Z`.
4. Tags `companion-vX.Y.Z`.

Inspect the diff and the CHANGELOG entry before pushing.

## 4. Push

```pwsh
git push origin main --follow-tags
```

`--follow-tags` is required; CI is triggered by the tag, not the branch
push. Confirm the workflow run starts under the **Actions** tab.

## 5. Verify the build

The workflow uses `tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0` and uploads:

- Windows NSIS `.exe` (installMode: `currentUser`, no UAC).
- macOS universal `.dmg`.
- Linux `AppImage` + `.deb` (`libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0` deps).

Smoke test:

- Download the artifact for your OS from the GitHub Release.
- Install / open. Tray icon appears.
- Press `Ctrl+Alt+M` → window toggles.
- Pair via OAuth device flow against the prod URL.
- Hub WebSocket connects (check `/devices` in the web app).
- Send a notification from `/conductor` → OS notification fires.

## 6. Roll back if needed

If the release is broken:

```pwsh
git tag -d companion-vX.Y.Z
git push origin :refs/tags/companion-vX.Y.Z
```

Delete the GitHub Release manually. Do NOT re-tag the same version with
different content — bump the patch and release again.

## Auto-updater

`createUpdaterArtifacts: false` — there is no auto-updater yet. Users
re-download installers manually until that's wired.

## Things to know

- Icons are auto-generated by `scripts/ensure-companion-icon.mjs`. Commit a
  real `apps/companion/src-tauri/icons/icon.png` to override.
- macOS notarization / code signing isn't set up yet — releases are
  unsigned. Mention this in the release notes for now.
- The release script only inspects commits scoped to companion-relevant
  packages. If you changed something elsewhere that the companion depends
  on, edit the CHANGELOG manually before tagging.
